mnemonics.title: Remembering Basic ls Options
mnemonics.content: LASHTR: L-long, A-all, S-size, H-human, T-time, R-recursive. Think: 'The LASH TRap catches all files!'

mnemonics.title: File Type Indicators
mnemonics.content: F: Directory = Door (/) | Executable = Star (*) | Link = At (@) | Socket = Equal (=). Think: 'DDEAS' = Directory, Directory, Executable, At, Socket.

acronyms.usage: Long listing format showing detailed file information
Shows all files including hidden files starting with dot
Appends type indicator characters to filenames
Combined: detailed listing with human-readable sizes

visualTips.visualization: Think of ls -l output as a table: Permission | Links | Owner | Group | Size | Date | Name. The first character is the file type key (d=door/directory, -=regular).
Flags stack together: ls -lah = list all (a) files in long (l) human-readable (h) format. Like building blocks!
Time t, Size S, Reverse r. Remember: 'Time Size Reverse' - like TSR in computing! Or think: 'tS r' sounds like 'teaser'.

analogies.analogy: ls is like opening a folder in a file explorer - it shows you what's inside a directory.
Like showing invisible files in Windows - they exist but are hidden by default to prevent accidental changes.
It's like getting a detailed inventory list: who owns each item, its size, when it was last touched, and its type.
It's like exploring every room and closet in a house, then every room in every house on a street.
Like putting labels on items: folders get a 'DIR' tag, programs get a 'RUN' tag, shortcuts get a 'LINK' tag.
